id.description: Id of the asset in the namespace's id scheme.

ns.description: Ns is the source namespace of the asset.

denied.description: Denied adds the deny entry when true and removes it when false.

kid.description: Kid is the key identifier carried in minted tokens.

not_after.description: NotAfter is the end of the validity window, unix seconds.

not_before.description: NotBefore is the start of the validity window, unix seconds.

secret.description: Secret is the hex-encoded HMAC key material.

use.description: Use is "delivery" for routinely rotated delivery keys or "public" for the long-lived key that signs permanent public URLs.

id.description: Id of the asset in the namespace's id scheme.

ns.description: Ns is the source namespace of the asset.

pin_selector.description: PinSelector pins the asset to this selector string at the edge (e.g. "c-0.2-0.2-0.5-0.5"). Optional.

version.description: Version narrows the purge to a single version. Optional; the default purges every version.

deleted_objects.description: DeletedObjects is the number of derivatives removed from the bucket.

invalidation_id.description: InvalidationId of the CDN invalidation, empty when the service runs without a CDN distribution configured.

id.description: Id of the asset in the namespace's id scheme.

ns.description: Ns is the source namespace of the asset.

version.description: Version of the asset; defaults to the current version ("0").

state.description: State is "queued" (population is pending, including when it already was), "cached" (an intermediate already exists), or "disabled" (the namespace doesn't use the intermediate cache — not an error, so fire-and-forget callers stay quiet across config changes).

post.description: GetSigningKeys returns the current signing key set: active, upcoming (pre-distributed before their not_before), and recently expired keys. Signers cache the set and select the delivery key whose validity window covers the current time.
RemoveSelectorPin removes a selector pin. Safe once every token minted before the pin was set has expired (the maximum token lifetime is 30 days) and issuance-time enforcement is in place.
SetAudienceDeny adds or removes an audience deny-list entry at the edge: coarse revocation of every URL signed for the audience.

Takedown purges an asset's derivatives from the derivatives bucket and invalidates its CDN paths. Optionally pins the asset to a single selector at the edge (the retroactive-crop kill switch): with a pin set, requests for any other selector are rejected regardless of token validity.

WarmIntermediate queues population of the cached intermediate for an asset, so the first derivative request doesn't pay the original fetch. Fire-and-forget: the response reports queueing, not completion. Requires the asset_warm or asset_admin scope.

tags.description: Keys serves the asset CDN signing keys to URL-minting services. The asset service is the key authority: it generates and rotates the keys, and both signers and the CDN edge get key material from it, so they can never disagree. Callers authorize with normal client credentials — a scope in their access token grants the key-fetch method, nothing more.
tags.name: Keys

tags.description: Management exposes the take-down operations: one authorized call instead of a runbook of raw AWS actions, callable by editorial tooling or event handlers as well as by humans. Note that in the ingest model a take-down only stops new deliveries — customers already hold ingested copies, so the take-down workflow is primarily a customer-notification process with this RPC as one step.
tags.name: Management
